chapter_title.ar: باب تَحْلِيلِ الْمُطَلَّقَةِ لِزَوْجِهَا وَ مَا يَهْدِمُ الطَّلَاقَ الْأَوَّلَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 80 – The Permissibility of the divorced woman to her husband and what demolishes the first divorce

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح وآخره مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (0/184)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح وآخره مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (0/184)
text: كَتَبَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ إِلَى أَبِي الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) رَوَى بَعْضُ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فِي الرَّجُلِ يُطَلِّقُ امْرَأَتَهُ عَلَى الْكِتَابِ وَ السُّنَّةِ فَتَبِينُ مِنْهُ بِوَاحِدَةٍ فَتَزَوَّجُ زَوْجاً غَيْرَهُ فَيَمُوتُ عَنْهَا أَوْ يُطَلِّقُهَا فَتَرْجِعُ إِلَى زَوْجِهَا الْأَوَّلِ أَنَّهَا تَكُونُ عِنْدَهُ عَلَى تَطْلِيقَتَيْنِ وَ وَاحِدَةٌ قَدْ مَضَتْ فَوَقَّعَ ( عليه السلام ) بِخَطِّهِ صَدَقُوا

وَ رَوَى بَعْضُهُمْ أَنَّهَا تَكُونُ عِنْدَهُ عَلَى ثَلَاثٍ مُسْتَقْبِلَاتٍ وَ أَنَّ تِلْكَ الَّتِي طَلَّقَهَا لَيْسَتْ بِشَيْ‏ءٍ لِأَنَّهَا قَدْ تَزَوَّجَتْ زَوْجاً غَيْرَهُ فَوَقَّعَ ( عليه السلام ) بِخَطِّهِ لَا .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Mahziyar who said,

‘Abdullah Bin Muhammad wrote to Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> of a report of some of our companions, from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding the man who divorces his wife upon the Book and the Sunnah, so she becomes irrevocably divorced from him with one (divorce). So she marries another husband, and he dies from her, or divorces her, she can return to her former husband, for her to be with him upon two divorces and one (of it) having expired.’ So he<sup>asws</sup> signed with his<sup>asws</sup> own handwriting: ‘They speak the truth’.

And some of them had reported that she would happen to be with him upon three future divorces, and that which he had divorced her with would not be with anything, because she had married another husband. So he<sup>asws</sup> signed with his<sup>asws</sup> own handwriting: ‘No’.
en.sarwar: 6. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn Mahziyar from who has said the following: “‘Abd Allah ibn Muhammad once wrote to abu al-Hassan, Alayhi al-Salam, saying that certain persons of our people have narrated from abu ‘Abd Allah, Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who divorces his wife according to the book and Sunnah and she becomes stranger for him after the first divorce; then marries another man who dies or divorces her and she goes back to her first husband to live with him for the two remaining divorces when one (divorce) has passed. He (the Imam) signed with his handwriting, ‘What they have narrated is true.’ A certain ones of them have narrated that she can live with him for three divorces in future and this divorce is not counted because she married another husband. He (the Imam) signed the answer in his handwriting, ‘No, that is not the case.”’
